name: Alpens, village of blacksmiths - circular tour through Lluçanès
name_original: Alpens, poblado de forjadores - circular por el Lluçanès
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Sant Agustí De Lluçanès traverse a landscape characterized by mountainous terrain, expansive pastures, and extensive forests of Scots pine and oak. The region features rural roads with minimal traffic, offering varying degrees of difficulty and elevation gains. This area in the Osona region of Catalonia provides a diverse backdrop for cycling, from routes with moderate elevation to more challenging rides. Best touring cycling routes around Sant Agustí De Lluçanès The most popular touring cycling route is Alpens, village of blacksmiths - circular tour through Lluçanès, a 14.3 miles (23.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 53 minutes to complete. This route offers a circular journey through the Lluçanès region. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Camí dels Munts – Santa Eulàlia de Puig-Oriol loop from Alpens, a difficult 27.5 miles (44.2 km) path. This route includes significant elevation gain and panoramic views of the surrounding mountainous landscape. Local touring cyclists also love the Sant Miquel de Gallifa – Camí dels Munts loop from Sant Quirze de Besora, a 27.2 miles (43.7 km) trail leading through forested areas and rural roads, often completed in about 3 hours 20 minutes. Touring cycling around Sant Agustí De Lluçanès is defined by mountainous terrain, open pastures, and dense Scots pine and oak forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to difficult routes with significant climbs. The routes in Sant Agustí De Lluçanès are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from around 50 reviews. More than 1,200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Sant Agustí De Lluçanès's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Sant Agustí De Lluçanès?
A: There are over 30 touring cycling routes around Sant Agustí De Lluçanès. These routes traverse a diverse landscape, offering options for various skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the touring cycling routes in this region?
A: The routes in Sant Agustí De Lluçanès feature mountainous terrain, expansive pastures, and extensive forests of Scots pine and oak. You'll find a mix of well-paved and some unpaved rural roads with minimal traffic, providing a picturesque backdrop for your ride.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly touring cycling routes?
A: While many routes are moderate to difficult, the region does offer paths suitable for varying fitness levels. For a moderate circular journey through the Lluçanès region, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976430346/alpens-village-of-blacksmiths-circular-tour-through-llucanes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alpens, village of blacksmiths - circular tour through Lluçanès&lt;/a&gt;, which is 14.3 miles long.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints I can visit while cycling?
A: The region is rich in points of interest. You can cycle towards the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2947681" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Mare de Déu dels Munts&lt;/a&gt;, which offers exceptional 360-degree panoramic views. Other historical sites include the ruins of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4074994" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Besora Castle&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2815824" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Martí Xic Chapel&lt;/a&gt;, both providing beautiful vistas from their elevated positions.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976430346/alpens-village-of-blacksmiths-circular-tour-through-llucanes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alpens, village of blacksmiths - circular tour through Lluçanès&lt;/a&gt;. For a more challenging circular ride with significant elevation, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38232689" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camí dels Munts – Santa Eulàlia de Puig-Oriol loop from Alpens&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Sant Agustí De Lluçanès?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from around 50 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from lush forests to mountains, and the quiet rural roads that allow for an immersive experience in nature.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Sant Agustí De Lluçanès?
A: The diverse landscapes of Sant Agustí De Lluçanès make it appealing throughout much of the year. However, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant weather conditions for cycling, with milder temperatures and vibrant natural scenery.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by natural water features?
A: Yes, you can find natural water features in the vicinity. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3045980" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riera de Sorreigs Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is a restored natural space nearby, offering an exceptional environment for nature lovers and walks amidst impressive landscapes.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes in Sant Agustí De Lluçanès?
A: While specific parking locations for each route are best checked on individual tour pages, the rural nature of Sant Agustí De Lluçanès and its surrounding villages often provides accessible parking options near trailheads or in town centers. Look for designated parking areas in villages like Alpens or Sant Quirze de Besora, which are common starting points for many routes.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. The region offers several difficult routes with significant elevation gains, some requiring very good fitness with over 3,000 feet of climbing. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10474173" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Miquel de Gallifa – Camí dels Munts loop from Sant Quirze de Besora&lt;/a&gt;, a 27.2-mile trail, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40341982" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camí dels Munts – Santa Eulàlia de Puig-Oriol loop from Sant Quirze de Besora&lt;/a&gt;, which is over 30 miles long.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay near the cycling routes?
A: The municipality is composed of scattered farmhouses, maintaining a traditional agricultural and livestock activity. This means you can often find local cuisine and rural accommodation options in the villages and hamlets throughout the Lluçanès area, perfect for cyclists looking to experience the local atmosphere.

Q: What is the typical duration for touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Route durations vary significantly based on distance and difficulty. For instance, a moderate 14.3-mile route like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976430346/alpens-village-of-blacksmiths-circular-tour-through-llucanes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alpens, village of blacksmiths - circular tour through Lluçanès&lt;/a&gt; can take under 2 hours, while more challenging 27-mile routes, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10474173" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Miquel de Gallifa – Camí dels Munts loop from Sant Quirze de Besora&lt;/a&gt;, might take over 3 hours.
tips: Sant Genís de Pi is a church of Sant Agustí de Lluçanès (Osona) included in the Inventory of the Architectural Heritage of Catalonia. You can get there from Alou.

Municipality belonging to the province of Barcelona located in the northern area of Llusanés, within the region of Osona.
It is also known as the town of forgers due to the forger artist, Joan Prat. He dedicated a large part of his work to the people who saw him born. The entire town is decorated with wrought iron, from the school fence to the rubbish in the streets.
